primary_diagnosis.name: Acute panic attack mimicking thyrotoxicosis, on a background of subclinical Graves' disease

comorbidities.name: Subclinical Graves' disease (suppressed TSH, normal free hormones, TSH-receptor antibodies)

pathophysiology_summary: Subclinical Graves' disease (suppressed TSH with normal free thyroid hormones and TSH-receptor antibodies) is a mildly overactive thyroid state that is physically stable and monitored. A stressful event triggered an adrenergic panic attack — palpitations, fine tremor, breathlessness and a sense of doom — which mimics thyrotoxicosis. The genuine emergencies to screen are a dangerous arrhythmia (SVT/rapid AF: very fast >150 or irregularly irregular, syncope), acute coronary syndrome (crushing/radiating chest pain) and thyroid storm (fever, delirium, extreme agitation) — all absent, with a regular sinus tachycardia (~105) and a clear psychological trigger. Telehealth cannot do an ECG, so a regular-rate, no-storm, no-ACS picture is de-escalated with a safety-net and routine TFTs.

differential.evidence_basis: Adrenergic symptoms after a stressful trigger with a fine tremor and prior anxiety.
Screen for a very fast/irregular rate and syncope; regular ~105 argues against.
Screen for crushing/radiating chest pain; absent.
Excluded by afebrile state, clear mentation and stable subclinical disease.
Regular ~105 with no syncope (DI-001), no ACS features and rest onset after a trigger (DI-002), afebrile with clear mentation (DI-003) — a panic attack.
Regular ~105 with no syncope; a dangerous tachyarrhythmia is not suggested (safety-net retained).
Afebrile, clear mentation, stable subclinical disease.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: a brief arrhythmia/ACS/storm screen, then confident de-escalation of a low-risk panic attack with paced breathing, routine TFTs, and a cardiac/arrhythmia/storm safety-net. Both reflex ambulance and unscreened dismissal are errors.

red_flags.description: Dangerous arrhythmia — very fast (>150)/irregularly irregular pulse or syncope
red_flags.clinical_significance: SVT/rapid AF may need urgent treatment.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed tachyarrhythmia can cause haemodynamic compromise.

red_flags.description: Acute coronary syndrome — crushing/radiating chest pain
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed ACS can be fatal.

red_flags.description: Thyroid storm — fever, delirium, extreme agitation
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed thyroid storm is life-threatening.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Assuming thyrotoxicosis/storm because of the known thyroid diagnosis, without screening the actual arrhythmia/ACS/storm features.
Dispatching an ambulance for a regular sinus tachycardia panic attack with a clear trigger.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: If regular-rate/no-storm/no-ACS: paced breathing, reassurance and routine TFTs, with a cardiac/arrhythmia/storm safety-net; escalate for red flags.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: False reassurance could miss an arrhythmia/ACS; over-triage reinforces catastrophic anxiety.
